headline: A Bash script hidden in plain sight on a T-shirt
lede: Uniqlo’s latest campaign prints a functional, obfuscated shell script across the front of its apparel. Decoding the Base64 sequence reveals a rhythmic, terminal-based animation that pays silent homage to the open-source foundations of the modern internet.

headline: GitHub AI agents tricked into leaking private repository data
lede: Researchers have demonstrated that agentic workflows often fail to distinguish between system instructions and untrusted user input. By exploiting this ambiguity, attackers can bypass security boundaries to exfiltrate protected information from private codebases.

headline: Building a minimal ZFS storage system on Debian
lede: Forget bloated storage appliances that hide functionality behind complex, proprietary GUIs. This approach leverages standard Linux administration tools and the robust ZFS file system to create a transparent, portable, and highly resilient home NAS.

headline: Hidden administrative backdoor discovered in Tenda router firmware
lede: A hardcoded authentication bypass in the binary firmware of multiple Tenda devices grants full administrative control to unauthorized actors. Without a manufacturer patch, owners must manually restrict network access to mitigate the risk of remote exploitation.

headline: GeoSQL brings spatial intelligence to AI development workflows
lede: This open-source plugin enables local-first spatial queries against major database engines without compromising credentials. Its unique map-in-the-loop validation architecture helps agents self-correct complex geometry errors in real-time.

headline: Preserving data from aging and fragile floppy disk media
lede: As magnetic storage media decays, specialized flux-level capture is required to salvage historical data trapped on obsolete floppies. A new community-vetted guide outlines the precise hardware and methodology needed to ensure these bytes survive the digital age.

headline: Revisiting the foundational SICP lectures from 1986
lede: DThese digitized recordings capture the seminal MIT curriculum on functional programming and system design. For students of computational abstraction, they remain an essential pedagogical resource that transcends the evolution of modern language syntax.

headline: The ongoing battle over EU Chat Control surveillance
lede: Legislative efforts to force the scanning of private encrypted messages continue to clash with fundamental digital privacy protections. The outcome of this policy debate threatens to permanently alter the architecture of end-to-end communication systems.
pullquote: The mandatory scanning of encrypted traffic inevitably undermines the very privacy that secure communication is intended to provide.

headline: GAO finds DOE prematurely ignores low-cost cleanup options
lede: Rigid planning cycles and contractor influence are driving up costs across the Department of Energy’s environmental cleanup programs. Independent audits suggest that fixing these systemic failures requires mission statements that remain neutral toward specific technologies.

headline: Canada’s last dedicated watchmaking school faces an uncertain future
lede: After eighty years of training precision mechanics, the École nationale d’horlogerie must relocate its operations. This institution stands as one of the few remaining bastions for a craft that prioritizes mechanical longevity over the convenience of the digital era.
